reasoning: Wdr48 (also called UAF1) functions mainly as a regulatory subunit for the deubiquitinases USP1, USP12 and USP46, modulating DNA‑damage response, PCNA de‑ubiquitination and cell‑cycle progression. Its known targets are proteins involved in replication and repair rather than transcription factors of the ETS family. Etv3 is an ETS‑domain transcriptional repressor whose expression is primarily controlled by MAPK signaling and transcriptional networks, with no reported interaction with the USP1‑UAF1 complex or other Wdr48‑associated pathways. Because there is no established mechanistic link linking Wdr48 activity to Etv3 transcription or stability, knocking down Wdr48 by CRISPRi is unlikely to cause a consistent up‑ or down‑regulation of Etv3.
